2606.06065.title: Multi-task Learning is Not Enough: Representational Entanglement in Dual-output Second Language Speech Recognition
2606.06065.paper_summary: Second-language (L2) speech recognition often requires transcriptions of pronunciations and intended meanings. Multi-task learning (MTL) is a natural approach because it assumes that shared representations benefit both outputs. However, this paper shows that this assumption does not hold across Korean and English. MTL improves meaning but degrades surface transcription, especially in English, where the degradation scales with surface-meaning divergence measured by Levenshtein edit distance.Encoder analysis links these patterns to encoder-level entanglement, with Korean preserving distinct task representations while English produces nearly identical ones. Cross-task decoder analysis shows that the meaning dual-output decoder adapts with a unique representation, while the surface dual-output decoder remains constrained by the encoder. These findings motivate the design of MTL frameworks that mitigate encoder-level entanglement to reduce surface degradation in dual-output L2 automatic speech recognition.
2606.06065.paper_summary_zh: 第二語言（L2）語音識別通常需要對發音和意圖的轉錄。多任務學習（MTL）是一種自然的方法，因為它假設共享的表示對兩個輸出都有益。然而，本文顯示這一假設在韓語和英語之間並不成立。MTL 改善了意義，但降低了表面轉錄的質量，特別是在英語中，表面意義的偏差與 Levenshtein 編輯距離測量的降級成正比。編碼器分析將這些模式與編碼器級的纏結聯繫起來，韓語保留了明確的任務表示，而英語則產生幾乎相同的表示。跨任務解碼器分析顯示，意義雙輸出解碼器以獨特的表示進行適應，而表面雙輸出解碼器則受到編碼器的限制。這些發現促使設計 MTL 框架，以減輕編碼器級的纏結，從而減少雙輸出 L2 自動語音識別中的表面降級。
2606.06065.author: Seung Hwan Cho et.al.
